title: 1. HTML Basics and Document Structure
reading:
What is HTML?

HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for creating Web pages. HTML elements are the building blocks of HTML pages.

💡 Note: HTML describes the structure of a Web page semantically.

A Simple HTML Document

<!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Page Title</title> </head> <body> <h1>My First Heading</h1> <p>My first paragraph.</p> </body> </html>

Explanation of the Example

  • <!DOCTYPE html>: Defines that this document is an HTML5 document.
  • <html>: The root element of an HTML page.
  • <head>: Contains meta information about the page.
  • <title>: Specifies a title for the page, shown in the browser's title bar or page tab.
  • <body>: Defines the document's body, a container for all visible contents.

quiz.q: What does HTML stand for?
Which element represents the root of an HTML document?
Where does the <title> element go?

title: 2. HTML Elements, Attributes, and Headings
reading:
HTML Elements

An HTML element is defined by a start tag, some content, and an end tag: <tagname>Content goes here...</tagname>

Nested HTML Elements: Elements can contain other elements. All HTML documents consist of nested HTML elements.

HTML Attributes

Attributes provide additional information about HTML elements. They are always specified in the start tag and usually come in name/value pairs like: name="value".

💡 Note: Always quote attribute values. Double quotes are the most common.

HTML Headings

HTML headings are defined with the <h1> to <h6> tags. <h1> defines the most important heading, while <h6> defines the least important.

<h1>Heading 1</h1> <h2>Heading 2</h2> <h3>Heading 3</h3>

quiz.q: Which tag defines the most important heading?
Where are attributes placed in an HTML element?
What format do attributes typically follow?

title: 3. HTML Links and Images
reading:
HTML Links

HTML links are hyper-links. You can click on a link and jump to another document.

Links are defined with the <a> tag:

<a href="https://www.w3schools.com/">Visit W3Schools.com!</a>

The href attribute specifies the destination address of the link.

HTML Images

Images are not technically inserted into a web page; images are linked to web pages. The <img> tag creates a holding space for the referenced image.

<img src="img_girl.jpg" alt="Girl in a jacket" width="500" height="600">
💡 Note: The alt attribute provides an alternate text for an image, if the user for some reason cannot view it (because of slow connection, an error in the src attribute, or if the user uses a screen reader).

quiz.q: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?
Which attribute specifies the URL of the link?
Which attribute is used to provide alternate text for an image?

title: 4. HTML Lists and Tables
reading:
HTML Lists

HTML offers two primary types of lists: Unordered and Ordered.

  • Unordered List: Starts with the <ul> tag. Each list item starts with the <li> tag. By default, items are marked with bullets.
  • Ordered List: Starts with the <ol> tag. Each list item starts with the <li> tag. By default, items are marked with numbers.
<ul> <li>Coffee</li> <li>Tea</li> </ul>

HTML Tables

An HTML table consists of one <table> element and one or more <tr>, <th>, and <td> elements.

💡 Note: <tr> defines a table row, <th> defines a table header, and <td> defines a table data/cell.
<table> <tr> <th>Company</th> <th>Contact</th> </tr> <tr> <td>Alfreds Futterkiste</td> <td>Maria Anders</td> </tr> </table>

quiz.q: Which tag defines an unordered list?
Which tag is used for a table row?
What does <th> stand for?

title: 5. HTML Forms and Inputs
reading:
HTML Forms

The <form> element is used to create an HTML form for user input. It acts as a container for different types of input elements, such as text fields, checkboxes, radio buttons, submit buttons, etc.

The <input> Element

The most used form element is the <input> element. It can be displayed in many ways, depending on the type attribute.

  • <input type="text"> - Defines a single-line text input field
  • <input type="radio"> - Defines a radio button (for selecting one of many choices)
  • <input type="submit"> - Defines a submit button (for submitting the form)

The <label> Element

💡 Note: The <label> element is useful for screen-reader users. It also helps users who have difficulty clicking on very small regions (like radio buttons or checkboxes) because clicking on the text within the label toggles the radio button/checkbox.
<form action="/submit"> <label for="fname">First name:</label><br> <input type="text" id="fname" name="fname"><br> <input type="submit" value="Submit"> </form>

quiz.q: Which element is used to create a form?
Which input type is used for a submit button?
What is the purpose of the <label> tag?
opts: To bind text to an input for accessibility

title: 6. Introduction to CSS and Selectors
reading:
What is CSS?

CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It describes how HTML elements are to be displayed on screen, paper, or in other media. CSS saves a lot of work by controlling the layout of multiple web pages all at once.

CSS Syntax

A CSS rule consists of a selector and a declaration block:

h1 {
color: blue;
font-size: 12px;
}

The selector (h1) points to the HTML element you want to style. The declaration block contains one or more declarations separated by semicolons. Each declaration includes a CSS property name and a value, separated by a colon.

CSS Selectors

CSS selectors are used to "find" (or select) the HTML elements you want to style.

  • Element Selector: Selects elements based on the element name. (e.g., p { ... })
  • Class Selector: Selects elements with a specific class attribute. Starts with a period character. (e.g., .center { ... })
  • ID Selector: Selects an element with a specific id attribute. Starts with a hash character. (e.g., #header { ... })
💡 Note: An id must be unique within a page, so the id selector is used to select one unique element!

quiz.q: What does CSS stand for?
How do you select an element with id 'demo'?
How do you select an element with class 'intro'?

title: 7. CSS Colors, Backgrounds, and Borders
reading:
CSS Colors

Colors in CSS are specified by valid color names (like red), HEX values (like #ff0000), RGB values (like rgb(255, 0, 0)), or HSL values.

p { color: tomato; }

CSS Backgrounds

The CSS background properties are used to add background effects for elements.

  • background-color: Specifies the background color of an element.
  • background-image: Specifies an image to use as the background.
  • background-repeat: Specifies if/how a background image will be repeated.
body { background-color: lightblue; }

CSS Borders

The CSS border properties allow you to specify the style, width, and color of an element's border.

💡 Note: The border-style property is required! If this property is not set, no border will be drawn.
div { border: 2px solid red; }

quiz.q: Which property changes the text color?
Which property changes the background color?
Which border property is absolutely required to show a border?

title: 8. CSS Box Model
reading:
The Box Model

All HTML elements can be considered as boxes. In CSS, the term "box model" is used when talking about design and layout.

The CSS box model is essentially a box that wraps around every HTML element. It consists of: margins, borders, padding, and the actual content.

  • Content: The content of the box, where text and images appear.
  • Padding: Clears an area around the content. The padding is transparent.
  • Border: A border that goes around the padding and content.
  • Margin: Clears an area outside the border. The margin is transparent.
💡 Note: When you set the width and height properties of an element with CSS, you just set the width and height of the content area. To calculate the full size of an element, you must also add padding, borders and margins.

Box Sizing

The box-sizing property solves the problem of calculating total width. By setting box-sizing: border-box;, padding and border are included in the element's total width and height.

* { box-sizing: border-box; }

quiz.q: What is the innermost part of the CSS Box Model?
Which property creates space inside the border, around the content?
What does `box-sizing: border-box` do?
opts: Includes padding and border in the total width and height

title: 9. CSS Text and Font Styling
reading:
Text Formatting

CSS has many properties to style text, making it readable and visually appealing.

  • text-align: Used to set the horizontal alignment of a text (e.g., center, left, right, justify).
  • text-decoration: Used to set or remove decorations from text. Commonly used to remove underlines from links (text-decoration: none;).
  • text-transform: Used to specify uppercase and lowercase letters in a text (e.g., uppercase, lowercase, capitalize).

CSS Fonts

The font-family property should hold several font names as a "fallback" system. If the browser does not support the first font, it tries the next font.

p { font-family: "Times New Roman", Times, serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: bold; }
💡 Note: Font family names with more than one word must be in quotation marks, like "Times New Roman".

quiz.q: Which property is used to remove the underline from links?
How do you make the text bold in CSS?
Which property aligns text horizontally?

title: 10. CSS Flexbox Layout
reading:
What is Flexbox?

The Flexible Box Layout Module, makes it easier to design flexible responsive layout structure without using float or positioning.

Flex Container

To start using the Flexbox model, you need to first define a flex container. This is done by setting the display property to flex.

.container { display: flex; }

Flex Properties

  • flex-direction: Defines in which direction the container wants to stack the flex items (row, column, row-reverse, column-reverse).
  • justify-content: Used to align the flex items horizontally across the main axis (e.g., center, space-between, space-around).
  • align-items: Used to align the flex items vertically across the cross axis (e.g., center, flex-start, flex-end).
💡 Note: Flexbox is primarily a 1-dimensional layout system, meaning it deals with either rows or columns, but not both at the same time.

quiz.q: How do you initialize a flexbox container?
Which property aligns items along the main axis?
What is the default flex-direction?

title: 11. CSS Grid Layout
reading:
What is CSS Grid?

The CSS Grid Layout Module offers a grid-based layout system, with rows and columns, making it easier to design web pages without having to use floats and positioning.

💡 Note: Unlike Flexbox (1-dimensional), CSS Grid is a 2-dimensional layout system, perfect for overall page layouts.

Grid Container

An element becomes a grid container when its display property is set to grid or inline-grid.

.grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto auto auto; gap: 10px; }

Grid Properties

  • grid-template-columns: Defines the number of columns and their widths in the grid layout.
  • grid-template-rows: Defines the height of each row.
  • gap: A shorthand property for row-gap and column-gap, setting the spacing between grid items.

quiz.q: CSS Grid is primarily a ___-dimensional layout system.
Which property defines the number and width of columns?
Which property sets the space between grid cells?

title: 12. Responsive Web Design and Media Queries
reading:
Responsive Web Design

Responsive Web Design is about using HTML and CSS to automatically resize, hide, shrink, or enlarge a website, to make it look good on all devices (desktops, tablets, and phones).

The Viewport

HTML5 introduced a method to let web designers take control over the viewport (the user's visible area of a web page), through the <meta> tag.

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

Media Queries

Media queries are a popular technique for delivering a tailored style sheet to different devices. It uses the @media rule to include a block of CSS properties only if a certain condition is true.

@media screen and (max-width: 600px) { body { background-color: lightblue; } }
💡 Note: The example above will apply the lightblue background ONLY if the browser window is 600px wide or smaller.

quiz.q: What does responsive web design aim to do?
opts: Make websites look good on all devices

quiz.q: Which HTML tag is used to control the viewport?
Which CSS rule is used for media queries?
